title: AI Engineering Intern

Part-time∙From May 1, 2026 to August 31, 2026
US work authorization required
Open to candidates with OPT/CPT
Job description

Do you stay up until 2 a.m. building AI agents?
Do you constantly think of new ways AI could automate things, save businesses time, or completely rethink how work gets done?

If that sounds like you, we want to meet you.

Alethia Software is a software development company supporting defense and national security customers. We are looking for an AI-focused intern who is passionate about building practical AI solutions that actually help organizations operate better.

This internship is designed for builders — people who want real projects, not busywork.

You will work directly with our engineering team to experiment with and implement AI solutions that solve real business problems.

What You’ll Work On

Examples of projects you may work on include:

Building AI agents that automate repetitive workflows for businesses
Creating tools that use LLMs to analyze data, documents, or operational processes
Developing automation systems that save companies time and money
Helping organizations modernize their digital presence using AI tools
Prototyping internal AI tools that improve engineering or operational workflows
Researching and testing new AI capabilities and use cases

What Makes This Internship Different

We want you to leave this internship with a real portfolio of meaningful work, not just a line on your resume.

You will:

Work on projects for real organizations
Build AI tools that solve actual problems
Create portfolio-ready case studies
Gain experience working alongside a software company that supports defense and government programs

Whether you plan to:

pursue a career in AI engineering, or
eventually freelance or build your own AI automation business

we want to help you build the credibility and portfolio to make that possible.

Ideal Candidate

You might be a great fit if you:

Love experimenting with AI tools and automation
Are constantly building things just to see if they work
Have experience with Python, JavaScript, APIs, or AI frameworks
Have used tools like OpenAI, LangChain, AutoGPT, Zapier, Make, or similar
Are curious, self-directed, and enjoy solving problems

Formal experience matters less than curiosity and initiative.

If you’ve built cool AI projects on your own, we want to see them.

What You'll Gain

Hands-on experience building real AI solutions
Mentorship from experienced software engineers
Portfolio projects you can show employers or clients
Exposure to defense and national security software development
Opportunities to contribute ideas and lead experiments

How to Apply

Send us:

A short note about what AI projects you’ve built
Links to GitHub, demos, or examples
Anything interesting you’ve experimented with

We care more about what you've tried to build than what classes you've taken.

Colorado Springs, CO

Alethia builds the software layer between data and the people who act on it. We take complex, disparate data sources, such as tracking feeds, telemetry, RF, compliance data, and sensor outputs, and turn them into clean, fast, usable interfaces that let operators and analysts do their jobs without fighting their tools.
